spec_match.rationale: This concept is a static comparison layout page rather than a functional implementation of the API.

interactive.rationale: The page is completely static, containing no interactive elements or simulations.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The content aligns with the spec by explaining the security and user gesture trust improvements of <usermedia>.

edge_cases.rationale: Since there is no interactive behavior or code execution, edge cases do not apply.

references.rationale: It lists links to ChromeStatus and MDN getUserMedia.

design_system.rationale: The layout uses the system styles, structured tables, and responsive columns.

openQuestions.title: Add interactive permission flow simulator
openQuestions.detail: Concept pages should be genuinely interactive, not just static code cards or tables. An interactive comparison showing step-by-step how a simulated prompt is blocked or allowed under both paradigms would significantly improve this concept.

summary: A well-designed static comparison explaining the user experience benefits of the declarative approach, but completely lacks interactivity which is a core rule of showcase concepts.
